Make the debate "should _we_ go to war" not "should we send other people's children to war." I like Israel's model of mandatory service.
Requiring every American household to sacrifice directly for war helps the public better understand it's costs. Sometimes it's worth it.

"Let the kids be happy" sounds like common sense, but it seems it has not been a priority in any decision related to children/schools/etc.
All this energy is spent corralling and controlling kids to prepare for adulthood- we should just culturally focus on letting them be happy.
But it also has this beautiful glimmer that all vocations do when you have never experienced it first-hand. Enough mystery to be promising.
